translations.notes: Django settings.py centralises all config. Fastify equivalent is env.ts which reads and validates env vars. Secret key mandatory check in production mirrors Django's SECURITY WARNING. ALLOWED_HOSTS semicolon-split logic preserved exactly. USE_FAST_HASHER (MD5 for tests) replaced by PBKDF2_ITERATIONS=1 env var per HV-002 decision.

DATABASE_URL conditional logic (lines 83-100) preserved with identical branching: no URL → file-based SQLite, ':memory:'/file: → in-memory SQLite, URL → PostgreSQL. BR-006 (TIME_ZONE=UTC, USE_TZ=True) implemented via timezone:'+00:00' on all dialects and dialectOptions.useUTC:true on PostgreSQL. ValueError/SystemExit on bad config preserved as thrown Errors.

Django MIDDLEWARE stack translated to Fastify plugin registrations. SecurityMiddleware+XFrameOptionsMiddleware → @fastify/helmet. CommonMiddleware+ALLOWED_HOSTS → @fastify/cors with origin whitelist. CsrfViewMiddleware → SameSite=Strict cookie strategy (no HTML form CSRF in SPA). SessionMiddleware+AuthenticationMiddleware → @fastify/jwt + authenticate preHandler. DEBUG wildcard (ALLOWED_HOSTS=['*']) → origin:true in development.

Django ROOT_URLCONF include() pattern → Fastify route module registration under /api prefix. TM-001: each Django URL group (articles.urls, accounts.urls, comments.urls) becomes a TODO stub with the corresponding sprint reference. Only health route is live in this sprint (bootstrap contract).
No Django equivalent — new endpoint required by migration_plan.json validation checkpoint. GET /api/health → 200 { status: 'ok' }. Zero business logic.

Django @login_required + LOGIN_URL='/login' (BR-005) redesigned as Fastify preHandler using JWT. Django redirected unauthenticated users (302 to /login); REST equivalent is 401 Unauthorized (Angular handles client-side redirect). Only token verification in this sprint — User model lookup added in accounts module.

Django WSGI/ASGI entry points (wsgi.py, asgi.py) merged into a single server.ts. DB connectivity check at startup mirrors Django's ImproperlyConfigured exception on bad DB config. Exits with code 1 on DB failure (same as Django's SystemExit).

INSTALLED_APPS 3rd-party packages translated to npm dependencies: taggit → explicit join tables (handled per-module), django-extensions → devDependencies ts-node-dev, pytest → jest/ts-jest. Core packages: fastify + plugins (@fastify/helmet, @fastify/cors, @fastify/jwt, @fastify/cookie), sequelize + adapters (pg, sqlite3), dotenv.

No Django equivalent — TypeScript project configuration. strict:true + esModuleInterop for clean type safety. experimentalDecorators enabled for future Sequelize model decorators (sequelize-typescript).

Django test settings (USE_FAST_HASHER) → Jest config. ts-jest preset. Tests match *.spec.ts. server.ts excluded from coverage (entry point only). PBKDF2_ITERATIONS=1 to be set in test process.env, mirroring Django USE_FAST_HASHER=True shortcut.

All environment variables documented with Django equivalents. SECRET_KEY → JWT_SECRET, DATABASE_URL (preserved), ALLOWED_HOSTS → ALLOWED_ORIGINS, USE_FAST_HASHER → PBKDF2_ITERATIONS. HV-002 decision documented inline.

flagged_for_review.reason: CSRF strategy changed from CsrfViewMiddleware to SameSite=Strict cookie. The SameSite attribute is enforced at the Set-Cookie level (accounts module). If any non-SPA consumer (mobile app, CLI scripts) sends cross-origin requests, this strategy must be re-evaluated.
flagged_for_review.suggestion: Validate with the team that all clients are SPA/XHR only. If server-side rendering or non-browser clients appear later, add @fastify/csrf-protection as a complementary layer.

flagged_for_review.reason: Stub only — does NOT populate request.user. Any route that uses fastify.authenticate as preHandler and then reads request.user will fail until the accounts module completes its decorator.
flagged_for_review.suggestion: Accounts module (Sprint 3) must extend this plugin to decode the JWT payload and attach the User record to request.user. Declare the FastifyRequest interface augmentation in src/types/fastify.d.ts at that time.

decisions.description: mots de passe PBKDF2 compatible Django — pas de bcrypt. PBKDF2_ITERATIONS env var en lieu de BCRYPT_ROUNDS. Implémentation complète dans accounts module.
AUTH_PASSWORD_VALIDATORS non migrés (excluded) — règle configurée mais non effective dans le legacy. Décision datée 2026-04-13 maintenue.
